intro: Relbunium orinocense, also known as "Orinoco vine," is a type of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, and has been used to treat various ailments, including fever and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Relbunium orinocense include iridoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
